title: Public constants should be replaced with static read-only fields
messageFormat: Expose this public constant as a 'static readonly' field so its value is not baked into other assemblies.
summary: The value of a public const is copied into every assembly that references it at compile time. If the constant ever changes, consumers keep the old value until they are recompiled, which produces silent version skew across a solution.

A public static readonly field is resolved at run time, so updating the declaring assembly is enough for every caller to observe the new value. Reserve public const for values that are part of the language or protocol and can never change.
